settings.title: The Long Road Our Teachers Walked
settings.excerpt: Between our three teachers sit decades of practice and training across very different traditions.
settings.body:
The voice guiding you through a Stillpoint class carries years of practice behind it, and each of our teachers arrived by a different path.

None of them set out to teach in a straight line. They came to it through dance, through stillness, through years on the mat, and each brought that history with them into the room.

Three different roads

Lena trained first as a dancer before a long study of alignment based yoga, which is why her cues are so precise. She notices the small things, a dropped shoulder, a locked knee, that others let pass.

Tom came to the mat through meditation and brings a deep stillness to his slower classes. With him, the pauses between movements feel as important as the movements themselves.

Our newest teacher, Aisha, trained abroad over several years and leads our prenatal sessions with real care and knowledge. Expectant mothers are in steady, well-informed hands with her.

What this means for you

None of them teach from a script. What you receive in class is the distilled result of a long, ongoing practice of their own. A few things that follow from that:

  • Classes vary with the teacher, so it is worth trying a few.
  • They adapt to the room rather than running a fixed routine.
  • If you have an injury or a concern, they have likely met it before.

We think that shows. Come along to a couple of different classes and you will quickly find the teacher whose way of working suits you.
